skill.name: patent-assistant
skill.description: Assists R&D teams with patent technical disclosure drafting and patent/novelty search analysis; use when users ask to write a patent disclosure, structure an invention description, search related patents, or assess novelty.
skill.summary: Draft structured patent disclosures and search multiple patent databases to assess invention novelty before filing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 104 potential issues (risk score 100/100). After semantic evaluation, 52 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are all false positives from pattern matching on innocuous text in patent disclosure templates and documentation -- no cryptographic code exists. Network findings are legitimate patent database API calls (Google Patents, Lens.org, Espacenet, etc.) with hardcoded endpoints. File writes are expected document-generation output. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious intent detected. Risk level downgraded to LOW.

low_findings.title: User-controlled file output path
low_findings.description: The disclosure generator script writes generated content to a user-specified file path via argparse. The content is always a template-generated patent disclosure document with no user-controlled injection into the content body. This is expected behavior for a document generation CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Outbound network requests to external patent databases
low_findings.description: The patent search script makes HTTP requests to multiple external patent database platforms (Google Patents, Lens.org, Innojoy, Baidu Academic, Espacenet, CNIPA). All endpoints are hardcoded to legitimate patent search services. No user credentials or API keys are transmitted. This is core expected functionality for a patent search tool.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static analyzer false positive cascade: weak cryptographic algorithm pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 52 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across all 4 files. After review, every finding is a false positive. The pattern matcher triggered on innocuous strings in patent disclosure templates (e.g., keywords, features, description), Python docstrings, Markdown headings, and JSON audit metadata. No cryptographic code of any kind exists in this skill.

content.user_title: Draft Patent Disclosures and Search Prior Art
content.value_statement: R&D teams often struggle to transform informal technical descriptions into structured patent disclosures. This skill guides you through drafting complete disclosure documents and searching patent databases to assess novelty before filing.
actual_capabilities: Convert informal technical descriptions into structured patent disclosure documents with standard sections including title, field, background, summary, detailed description, and drawings
Guide users through an information-collection checklist to fill gaps in problem statement, prior art defects, core solution, key features, and beneficial effects
Search multiple patent databases (Google Patents, Lens.org, Espacenet, CNIPA, Innojoy, Baidu Academic) via a Python CLI script with parallel execution support
Rank patent search results by keyword similarity and produce feature-by-feature comparison tables against the user's key technical features
Generate post-draft optimization suggestions including claim-writing directions, alternative embodiments, and missing technical details to supplement
Suggest IPC classification codes and extract keywords with synonyms to improve patent search recall
limitations: Generated disclosures are drafting aids and require inventor review and technical supplementation before internal submission
Automated patent search results do not replace formal novelty searches conducted by professional patent search institutions
Claims drafting guidance is non-legal and all patent applications should be reviewed by a qualified patent attorney
Patent database CLI access may be limited by rate limiting, regional restrictions, or service unavailability

use_cases.title: R&D Engineer Drafting an Invention Disclosure
use_cases.description: An engineer with a novel technical solution transforms rough notes and verbal descriptions into a complete, structured patent disclosure document ready for internal IP review.

use_cases.title: IP Coordinator Running a Preliminary Patent Search
use_cases.description: An IP coordinator or patent liaison searches multiple patent databases simultaneously, produces similarity comparisons, and identifies the closest prior art references before engaging external patent counsel.

use_cases.title: Startup Founder Validating Invention Uniqueness
use_cases.description: A startup founder without formal patent expertise structures their invention description and runs preliminary patent searches to gauge novelty and decide whether to pursue patent protection.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Disclosure Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a patent disclosure document: [describe your invention in a few sentences including the problem, your solution, and what makes it different from existing approaches]
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a rough idea who needs a structured disclosure draft

prompt_templates.title: Guided Disclosure with Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to draft a complete patent technical disclosure. Ask me the key questions one at a time: what is the technical problem, what are the defects of existing solutions, what is the core idea of my solution, what are the key technical features, and what beneficial effects are achieved. Then generate the full disclosure document.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants interactive guidance to ensure all required sections are complete

prompt_templates.title: Patent Search by Technical Keywords
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for patents related to: [technical keywords]. Use multiple platforms. Compare the top results against my key technical features: [list features]. Rank the results by similarity and highlight which references are closest to my invention.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to find prior art and assess how close existing patents are to their invention

prompt_templates.title: Full Disclosure and Novelty Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an invention: [detailed description]. First, extract the key technical features and suggest IPC classifications. Second, compose search queries with synonyms. Third, search all available patent databases. Fourth, rank results by technical similarity and produce a feature-by-feature comparison. Finally, give me a preliminary novelty judgment and identify the closest prior art references.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants a comprehensive end-to-end disclosure draft and novelty analysis

output_examples.input: Help me write a patent disclosure for a system that uses on-device AI to detect motor vibration anomalies and only uploads event snippets.
output_examples.output: A structured markdown disclosure document with sections: Title of Invention, Technical Field, Background Art (description of existing technology and its defects), Summary of Invention (technical problem, solution, beneficial effects), Detailed Description with embodiments, Description of Drawings with figure suggestions, and Keywords. Each section contains specific technical content derived from the user's description.

output_examples.input: Search patents for 'vibration anomaly detection edge event-based upload' and compare results against my key features: on-device inference, stream compression, event-triggered upload.
output_examples.output: Results grouped by patent database source, with patent numbers, titles, abstracts, and similarity scores for each match. A feature-by-feature comparison table showing which of the user's three key features appear in each reference. A preliminary novelty judgment noting which references share the most features and represent the closest prior art.

output_examples.input: Review my disclosure draft and tell me what is missing or could be improved before I submit it to our IP team.
output_examples.output: A gap analysis identifying missing technical details such as data formats, interface specifications, model training constraints, and operating parameter ranges. Suggestions for claim-writing directions including independent claim scope and dependent claim fallbacks. Recommendations for alternative embodiments and expansion ideas to strengthen the disclosure.

best_practices: Provide as much specific technical detail as possible in your initial description for the most complete and accurate disclosure draft
Always review and supplement generated disclosure documents with your own technical knowledge and domain expertise before internal submission
Use patent search results as a preliminary screening tool to guide your filing strategy, not as a replacement for professional novelty searches
anti_patterns: Do not rely on AI-generated disclosures as final patent application text without thorough review and approval by a qualified patent attorney
Do not assume patent search results are exhaustive -- the tool searches publicly indexed databases and may miss non-indexed or freshly filed patents
Do not disclose invention details in public AI chat sessions or file patent applications without first confirming confidentiality obligations and internal disclosure policies with your organization

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps R&D teams draft structured patent technical disclosure documents and search multiple patent databases to assess invention novelty before filing.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice or replace a patent attorney?
faq.answer: No. The skill produces drafting aids and search results for internal use. All patent applications and legal decisions should be reviewed by a qualified patent attorney.

faq.question: Which patent databases can I search with the included tools?
faq.answer: The patent search script supports Google Patents, Lens.org, Dawei Innojoy, Baidu Academic, Espacenet (European Patent Office), and CNIPA (China National Intellectual Property Administration). You can search one or all platforms simultaneously.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: The disclosure drafting workflow works directly through the AI assistant. To run the patent search script, you need Python 3.9 or later with the requests, beautifulsoup4, and lxml packages.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee that my invention is novel and patentable?
faq.answer: No. The patent search is a preliminary screening tool. A formal novelty search by a professional patent search firm and legal analysis by a patent attorney are recommended before filing.

faq.question: What output format does the disclosure generator produce?
faq.answer: It produces markdown documents with standard patent sections including title, technical field, background art, summary of invention, detailed description with embodiments, drawing suggestions, and keywords.
